channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: The world welcomes 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — From Sydney to Mumbai to Nairobi, communities around the world welcomed 2025 with spectacular light shows, embraces and ice plunges.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly all of Puerto Rico is without power on New Year's Eve
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A blackout hit nearly all of Puerto Rico early on Tuesday as the U.S. territory prepared to celebrate New Year’s, leaving more than 1.3 million clients in the dark. Officials said it could take up to two days to restore power.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel kills Hamas commander who led heinous Oct. 7 attack on Kibbutz Nir Oz killed in drone attack: IDF
item.description: The Israel Defense Force announced that they had successfully killed one of the terrorist leaders who was responsible for the Oct. 7 attack on Israel in a targeted attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China's Xi cites global 'uncertainties' in speech ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping painted a picture of a strong Chinese economy in his New Year's speech, while also alluding to external "uncertainties" as President-elect Trump prepares to return to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Russian and Iranian groups over disinformation targeting American voters
item.description: The U.S. has imposed sanctions on two groups linked to Russian and Iranian disinformation campaigns targeting this year's election.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: At least 66 people die after a truck plunges into a river in southern Ethiopia
item.description: At least 66 people were killed in southern Ethiopia after an overcrowded truck that was contracted by wedding guests plunged into a river.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Dozens of patients and wounded evacuated from Gaza for treatment
item.description: Dozens of patients and the wounded have been evacuated for treatment outside the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, where the United Nations says Israel's attacks on and around hospitals have pushed health care to the brink.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A new year dawns on a Middle East torn by conflict and change
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — In Damascus, the streets were buzzing with excitement Tuesday as Syrians welcomed in a new year that seemed to many to bring a promise of a brighter future after the unexpected fall of Bashar Assad’s government weeks earlier.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED, WAFAA SHURAFA and LUJAIN JO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Berlin police say they have detained a man who attacked 2 people with a knife
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Berlin police said they detained a man Tuesday who attacked and injured two people in the city's Charlottenburg neighborhood, further jolting Germany days after a deadly Christmas market attack elsewhere in the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US levels sanctions on entities in Iran, Russia over attempted election interference
item.description: The Biden administration announced new sanctions against Russia and Iran on Tuesday in response to efforts by both countries to influence the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Lemon calls Trump supporters 'f‑‑‑ing idiots' amid H-1B visa infighting
item.description: Former CNN host Don Lemon poked fun at the Republican Party's infighting over H-1B visas — a key program to attract international talent that’s been criticized as too complicated and susceptible to abuse — calling supporters of President-elect Trump "dumb f‑‑‑ing idiots.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Palestinian official predicts Trump will 'destroy' Iran, leading to breakdown of remaining Hamas cells: report
item.description: Palestinian official Mohammad Hamdan said he believes President-elect Donald Trump will "destroy Iran" and effectively decimate Hamas' grasp on the region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Running a marathon a day in 2024, she has a world record within her grasp
item.description: GHENT, Belgium (AP) — Finally coming to a halt on Tuesday — the last day of 2024 — Belgian ultra runner Hilde Dosogne felt she had done all to take the world record as the first woman to run a marathon every single day of the year.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Montenegro extradites cryptocurrency mogul Do Kwon to the United States
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — Montenegro on Tuesday extradited a South Korean mogul known as “the cryptocurrency king” to the United States, following a decision of its justice ministry earlier this month to accept a U.S.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Berlin police say they have detained a man who attacked 2 people with a knife
item.description: Berlin police say they have detained a man who attacked and injured two people in the city’s Charlottenburg neighborhood, further jolting Germany days after a deadly Christmas market attack elsewhere in the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Longest-held Guantánamo Bay detainee sent back to Tunisia
item.description: A detainee at a military prison in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, has been repatriated to Tunisia, the Pentagon announced Monday. Ridah Bin Saleh al-Yazidi, 59, was eligible to transfer following an interagency review process, the release said. He was the longest-held detainee at the prison.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine: How the war shifted in 2024
item.description: A third year of war in Ukraine drags on as the new year and the conflict's February anniversary approach. The war in 2024 saw major shifts on the battlefield and in Western politics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US and Boeing investigators examine the site of a deadly South Korean plane crash
item.description: A team of U.S. investigators including representatives from Boeing have examined the site of a plane crash that killed 179 people in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zimbabwe's new currency woes hit traditional stores while illegal night bazaars flourish
item.description: People in Zimbabwe are shunning traditional grocery stores to do their shopping at informal and illegal markets that pop up at night to avoid police raids.
item.dc:creator: Farai Mutsaka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Serbian court convicts the parents of a teenage boy who shot dead 10 people in a school in Belgrade
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — A court in Serbia on Monday convicted the parents of a teenage boy who last year shot dead nine pupils and a school guard and wounded six more people in a school in central Belgrade.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia, Ukraine swap more than 300 prisoners
item.description: Russia and Ukraine announced Monday a swap of more than 300 prisoners of war. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced his team secured the return of 189 Ukrainians, while Russia announced the return of 150 service members.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Uncovering the atrocities of the Assad regime at its 'death factory' on the hill
item.description: Sednaya prison was the site where thousands of Syrians were tortured and killed under Assad’s regime. Investigators have uncovered mass graves, and are calling for justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean authorities seek warrant to detain impeached President Yoon in martial law probe
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean law enforcement officials requested a court warrant on Monday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol as they investigate whether his short-lived martial law decree on Dec. 3 amounted to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Gaza's crowded tent camps, women wrestle with a life stripped of privacy
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza City (AP) — For Gaza’s women, the hardships of life in the territory’s sprawling tent camps are compounded by the daily humiliation of never having privacy.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu completes prostate surgery after UTI diagnosis
item.description: The prostate surgery that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu underwent on Sunday has been completed, Fox News has learned. The Israeli leader suffered a UTI before the procedure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Azerbaijan's president says crashed jetliner was shot down by Russia unintentionally
item.description: Azerbaijan’s President Ilham Aliyev said Sunday that the Azerbaijani airliner that crashed last week was shot down by Russia, albeit unintentionally, and criticized Moscow for trying to “hush up” the issue for days. "We can say with complete clarity that the plane was shot down by Russia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China directs largest military build-up since 1930s Nazi Germany, expert warns, citing Pentagon report
item.description: Defense Department report reveals China's rapidly growing weapons arsenal and "psychological warfare" operation ahead of new Trump presidency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plane skids off runway in South Korea, killing at least 177
item.description: A passenger plane burst into flames Sunday after it skidded off a runway at a South Korean airport and slammed into a concrete fence when its front landing gear apparently failed to deploy.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mexico tests app for migrants to send alert if they're about to be detained in US
item.description: U.S. authorities are obliged to give notice to home-country consulates when a foreign citizen is detained. Mexico says it has beefed up consular staff and legal aid to help migrants in the legal process related to deportation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Aviation experts say Russia's air defense fire likely caused Azerbaijan plane crash as nation mourns
item.description: Aviation experts said Thursday that Russian air defense fire was likely responsible for the Azerbaijani plane crash the day before that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
